channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Multisite atomic-chlorine-passivation stabilizes perovskite interfaces for efficient H<sub>2</sub>O<sub>2</sub> photosynthesis from seawater
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70503-2

Lead halide perovskites are promising for artificial photosynthesis but are unstable in seawater. Here, the authors report multisite atomic-chlorine passivation to stabilize CsPbI3 quantum dots in a hydrophobic covalent organic framework, enabling efficient H2O2 photosynthesis directly from seawater.
item.dc:title: Multisite atomic-chlorine-passivation stabilizes perovskite interfaces for efficient H<sub>2</sub>O<sub>2</sub> photosynthesis from seawater
item.dc:creator: Genping Meng
Shuai Wei
Ning Li
Yuhui Yin
Bin Dong
Shihao Sun
Guowen Hu
Hao Wang
Baodui Wang

item.title: Flash joule heating-induced spinel-phase surface in Ni-rich layered oxide positive electrodes to stabilise lattice oxygen
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70616-8

High-nickel layered positive electrodes suffer from lattice oxygen loss and inherent lattice strain. Here, authors report a self-derived subtractive strategy to form a coherent spinel-phase shell with tunable thickness to confine active oxygen intermediates and suppresses interfacial side reactions.
item.dc:title: Flash joule heating-induced spinel-phase surface in Ni-rich layered oxide positive electrodes to stabilise lattice oxygen
item.dc:creator: Huiping Yang
Zhefei Sun
Yonghui Zhao
Ying Zhang
Zhiyi Sun
Hongling Yi
Huiqun Wang
Yuxiang Mao
Junjie Liu
Wenxing Chen
Jiexi Wang
Shijie Feng
Qinghe Zhao
Yang Cao
Jiajia Han
Qiaobao Zhang
Li Zhang

item.title: Giant ZT enhancement in rhombohedral GeTe-based thermoelectric materials
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70793-6

The thermoelectric performance of rhombohedral GeTe-based material is boosted by the TiB2 nanoparticle-induced interfacial constraint effect that results in reduced equivalent deformation potential and substantial interfacial thermal resistance.
item.dc:title: Giant ZT enhancement in rhombohedral GeTe-based thermoelectric materials
item.dc:creator: Jincheng Yu
Xiaodong Liu
Yilin Jiang
Chen Chen
Jing-Wei Li
Haihua Hu
Lin Song
Tian Xie
Bin-Bin Ruan
Yu Pan

B. Layla Mehdi
Xiaoyuan Zhou
Jing-Feng Li
